desc:
This narrow way twists and turns between the high stone walls of the buildings.
Piles of refuse and rubbish litter the ground, and emit a putrid stench of rot
and decay. Eastwards some small wooden docks provide landing spots for ships,
while Chapman's Lane continues its stroll northwards.
name: Chapman's Lane

desc:
The narrow lane makes its way between the buildings facing onto Main Street and
the Old Tower Road. The buildings' stout back doors carry heavy padlocks. Piles
of garbage decay beside the doors. A huge, green vine is crawling its way up the
wall on the side of the street and it might be possible to climb it to get up to
the roof.
name: Chapman's Lane

desc:
Here you have a very good view over the street below. People are walking up and
down the street minding their own business and not paying attention to what
happens above them. A huge vine grows on the back wall of this building
extending downward.
name: On the Rooftop

desc:
You have entered a small, dimly lit room. In the vague light from a lantern you
see all kinds of knives gleaming on the northern wall. A fully dressed mannequin
stands in the middle of the room and a complete black silk dress hangs over a
chair in the northeast corner. A very small ladder on the western wall leads up
to the roof, where it abruptly ends.
name: Humble Workshop

desc:
Here, high above the city, birds are circling around trying to find something to
eat or simply to stretch their wings. Below you the city is buzzing with life
and from here it looks quite harmless, however, it is easy to watch who walks on
the street below. The sheer walls fall steeply to the street and it would be
extremely difficult to try to climb down.
name: High above the City

desc:
From here you have a good view over the city. To the east an old tower can be
seen reaching high above the city. Seagulls and some other birds are circling
around it, squawking loudly. Massive city walls surround the city and you can
see the northern gates from here. It is possible to climb up the roof west of
you to reach even higher above the city.
name: Atop the Roof

desc:
Balancing atop the high roofs, it is possible to overlook the city, which
stretches out below your feet. Looking down, the course of the Old Tower Street
running west and south is clearly visible, while the massive stone wall of an
old tower is just some yards to the east.
name: On the Roofs

desc:
Above the roof birds circle and chirp trying to spot food or a good place to
build a nest. The roof is made of wooden planks and a small hatch opens to a
ladder leading below. To the northeast, the old tower of Tharbad reaches up into
the sky.
name: Upon the Rooftops

desc: You are falling down...
name: Off the Tower-roof

desc:
The cylindrical roof is built of reddish bricks, while the very top is a small
flat area of wooden, cracked planks. This is the highest place in Tharbad and
from here it is easy to overlook the whole city. To the east the River Greyflood
begins its journey; first a huge irregular area of branching rivulets, then
getting smaller and smaller, it vanishes into the horizon.
name: On the Tower-Roof

desc: You are falling down...
name: Off the Tower-roof

desc: You are falling down...
name: Off the Tower-roof

desc:
Here in the narrow corridor, the tapestries on the walls are old and withered.
Bits and pieces of them lie on the floor where cockroaches have taken to hiding
under them. At one end of the corridor, a small door opens to a dimly lit room
from which no details can be seen. Light comes from the bottom of the stairs
where the noise of the common patrons in the inn can be heard.
name: A Narrow Corridor

desc:
Dirty water flows westwards towards a wide stone bridge off in the distance.
Several pipes dump an abundance of russet coloured sewage into the river from
the buildings on the north and south banks, producing a ripe smell of sewage.
Notably, a wide path cuts between a few of the houses on the south bank.
name: The Greyflood

desc:
The wide stone bridge overhead provides little shelter from the elements. The
wind still whistles as it slips under the bridge and past the rocks at the
river's edge. As putrid water laps against the rocks, a faint sound of running
water can be heard coming from the northern bank.
name: Under the Bridge

desc:
A pungent odour rises from the river. The water is a concoction of sewage,
garbage, slime and unknown muck as it collects on the bank. The main part of the
river runs swiftly here, from the stone bridge towards the city gates in the
west.
name: The Greyflood

desc:
The current slows and fragments momentarily as the river becomes choked with
weed and debris, its force dispersed into small eddies and backwaters. Low banks
patched with small clumps of hardy dun-coloured grasses border the polluted
waterway as it flows towards the gated entrance to the west. A resident
occasionally adds to the current a helping of garbage or perhaps the contents of
a chamber-pot.
name: The Greyflood

desc:
Sheltered from the elements under the city's wide stone wall, this area serves
as a checkpoint for watercraft coming into and out of Tharbad. On each side of
the narrow waterway three large torches chained to the high arch of the walls
sway in the light breeze. Barely audible above the noise from the city echoing
in the arch, the dirty Greyflood murmurs and gurgles past a rusted metal gate.
name: Tharbad's West Gate

desc:
Discoloured water and debris slowly pour out from the large, rusted metal gate
just behind Tharbad's high stone wall, creating an opaque brown plume several
hundred yards long. Occasionally a mutilated corpse surfaces for a second or two
before diving beneath the murky waves to the unseen depths of the river.
name: Outside the West Gate of Tharbad

desc:
The water is polluted and murky. Half-disintegrated garbage litters the bottom
along with slimy moss which collects on the loamy riverbed. Vision is obscured
by the murkiness, and only the slight bumps from what feel like fishes can be
discerned.
name: Below the Water Surface

desc:
The entire building seems to be a large warehouse. Narrow aisles wind between
shelves piled to the rafters with crates and boxes. The only large spaces are at
the south and north ends of the room. To the south, two desks are facing a
doorway, and in the north there is a set of iron gates.
name: Warehouse

desc:
Dark, damp and extremely cramped, this small tunnel seems to have been created
by water flowing through a wide crack in the rocks on its way to the catacombs
below. Stalactites hang from the low ceiling, formed from centuries of dripping
water. These rock formations meet along the wall with stalagmites forming this
tunnel. The faint sound of flowing water can be heard from the south.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
Wooden docks provide landing facilities for ships. Ropes pile up, some of them
are tied to the docks and hang downwards, nearly reaching the water surface.
Looking through the narrow space between the planks below, small waves of dirty
and stinking water breaks against the riverbank.
name: On the Docks

desc:
The wooden planks creak while standing upon them. The aftmast is located here
and is nearly as tall as the mainmast. A huge grey sail in triangular shape
hangs between them. Standing close to the railing which surrounds the whole
ship, are some barrels.
name: The Aftdeck

desc:
Large wooden planks cover this part of the ship's deck. The main mast reaches
far into the sky and a huge grey sail hangs loosely between it and the rear
mast. Numerous oars are neatly stacked close to the wooden railing, which
surrounds the ship at this end. The oars might be used when there is no wind for
long periods of time or in emergency cases.
name: On the Grey Ship
